__notes.summary: LL-HLS via MediaMTX packager; target ~1–2s latency

flow.LL_HLS: Camera RTSP → FFmpeg Publisher (unified-nvr) → MediaMTX Packager → NGINX Edge → Browser (hls.js)
flow.HLS: Camera RTSP → FFmpeg Transcoder (unified-nvr) → NGINX Edge → Browser (hls.js)
flow.RTMP: Camera RTSP → FFmpeg Transcoder (unified-nvr) → NGINX Edge → Browser (flv.js)
flow.mjpeg_proxy: Camera (RTSP snapshots or HTTP) → Python Proxy (unified-nvr) → NGINX Edge → Browser ()

architecture.why_ll_hls_uses_publisher: Camera native GOP (typically 4-5s) would force MediaMTX to create equally long segments = high latency. We transcode to controlled GOP (0.5-1s) so MediaMTX can create sub-2s latency LL-HLS with 200ms partials. Other stream types transcode directly to final format.

input.RTSP_UDP: Lower latency, tolerates some packet loss, preferred for local network
input.RTSP_TCP: Higher latency but guaranteed delivery, use if UDP shows corruption errors

output.LL_HLS: RTSP/TCP or RTSP/UDP to MediaMTX (TCP stable but adds latency, UDP lower latency but may freeze)
output.HLS: Direct file write to /app/streams/ (no network protocol)
output.RTMP: Direct file write to /app/streams/ (no network protocol)
output.mjpeg_proxy: HTTP response to browser (no intermediate storage)

__notes.name: e.g. CAM OFFICE - Human-readable camera name displayed in UI
__notes.model: e.g. RLC-410-5MP - Camera model number for reference/troubleshooting
__notes.type: e.g. reolink - Vendor type, determines which handler class processes this camera (ReolinkService, UniFiProtectService, EufyService)
__notes.serial: e.g. REOLINK_OFFICE - Unique identifier used as key in active_streams dict, DOM data attributes, API endpoints
__notes.host: e.g. 192.168.10.88 - Camera IP address on local network
__notes.mac: e.g. ec:71:db:3e:93:f5 - MAC address for network troubleshooting/DHCP reservations

capabilities.ptz: Camera has Pan-Tilt-Zoom controls (not present on this model)
capabilities.audio: Camera has audio (not explicitly listed but available via RTSP)

stream_type.description: Determines which streaming pipeline to use
stream_type.LL_HLS: Use MediaMTX packager for Low-Latency HLS (this camera)
stream_type.HLS: Use app-generated HLS via FFmpeg (most cameras)
stream_type.RTMP: Use RTMP/FLV stream with flv.js player

hls_js.description: Configuration for hls.js library (used by HLS and LL_HLS streams)

enableWorker.description: Run demuxing in Web Worker (separate thread)
enableWorker.true: Better performance, offloads parsing from main thread
enableWorker.false: All processing on main thread, may cause UI jank on weak devices
enableWorker.recommendation: Always true unless debugging worker-related issues

lowLatencyMode.true: Requests playlist updates via blocking requests (_HLS_msn, _HLS_part params), processes partial segments
lowLatencyMode.false: Standard HLS - polls playlist every few seconds, waits for complete segments
lowLatencyMode.critical: Must be true for LL_HLS stream_type. Can be false for regular HLS stream_type.

liveSyncDurationCount.description: How many segments behind live edge to target
liveSyncDurationCount.tradeoff: Lower = less latency but more buffering risk. Higher = more latency but smoother playback.

liveMaxLatencyDurationCount.description: Maximum allowed latency before speed adjustment kicks in (in segments)
liveMaxLatencyDurationCount.behavior: If player falls more than max drift behind live, playback rate increases (up to maxLiveSyncPlaybackRate) to catch up
liveMaxLatencyDurationCount.tradeoff: Lower = aggressive catchup (may cause audio pitch changes). Higher = accepts more delay before correction.

maxLiveSyncPlaybackRate.description: Maximum playback speed multiplier for catching up to live edge
maxLiveSyncPlaybackRate.1.5: Play at 150% speed (1.5x) when behind - noticeable but acceptable
maxLiveSyncPlaybackRate.caveat: Higher values cause audio pitch changes (chipmunk effect), use sparingly

backBufferLength.description: How many seconds of video to keep in buffer behind current playback position
backBufferLength.tradeoff: Lower = less memory usage, less rewind. Higher = more memory, better seeking.

future_protocols.flv_js: Configuration for flv.js (RTMP streams) - not yet implemented
future_protocols.dash_js: Configuration for DASH streams if added in future
future_protocols.native_player: Settings for browsers with native HLS support (Safari) - currently uses defaults

how_applied.merge_strategy: Smart defaults based on stream_type (LL_HLS vs HLS), then override with camera-specific player_settings
how_applied.fallback: If player_settings missing or incomplete, uses hardcoded defaults appropriate for stream type

tuning_guide.smoother_playback: Increase liveSyncDurationCount (2-3), increase liveMaxLatencyDurationCount (4-5), decrease maxLiveSyncPlaybackRate (1.2)
tuning_guide.debugging_buffering: Increase all values by 1-2, check if issues resolve (indicates network/encoding bottleneck)

ll_hls.description: Complete configuration for LL-HLS publishing pipeline

publisher.description: Settings for FFmpeg output to MediaMTX

protocol.rtmp: Alternative, uses port 1935, tested working but not used

host.description: Docker Compose service name of MediaMTX container
host.network: Both containers on nvr-net bridge network, Docker DNS resolves service names

port.description: MediaMTX RTSP listener port (1935 for RTMP)
port.container_internal: Port is internal to Docker network, not exposed to host

path.description: Stream path in MediaMTX, must match packager_path and mediamtx.yml paths: section

rtsp_transport.tcp: Stable, adds ~1-2s latency, prevents freezing. CURRENTLY REQUIRED.
rtsp_transport.udp: Lower latency (~1s faster) but stream freezes within minutes. Root cause unknown, possibly packet loss or MediaMTX timeout.
rtsp_transport.critical: This is OUTPUT transport (FFmpeg→MediaMTX), separate from rtsp_input.rtsp_transport (Camera→FFmpeg)

video.description: FFmpeg video encoding parameters for publisher

c:v.why_not_copy: Must re-encode to control GOP size (camera GOP ~5s, we need 1s)
c:v.alternatives: libx265 (HEVC, better compression but higher CPU), h264_nvenc (NVIDIA GPU)

preset.range: ultrafast → superfast → veryfast → faster → fast → medium → slow → slower → veryslow
preset.tradeoff: Faster = lower CPU, worse compression. Slower = higher CPU, better quality at same bitrate.
preset.why_veryfast: Balance between CPU usage (~4-5% per stream) and quality

tune.description: Disables encoding features that add latency
tune.specifics: Disables B-frames (bidirectional prediction), reduces lookahead buffer, forces immediate frame output
tune.impact: Slightly worse compression but critical for live streaming low latency

profile:v.baseline: Maximum compatibility, no B-frames, works on all devices
profile:v.main: Better compression, allows B-frames (conflicts with zerolatency)
profile:v.high: Best compression but requires modern hardware
profile:v.why_baseline: Maximum browser compatibility + required for zerolatency tune

pix_fmt.yuv420p: 4:2:0 subsampling, standard for web video, widely supported
pix_fmt.yuv444p: 4:4:4 no subsampling, better quality but not supported by baseline profile

r.source_fps: Camera outputs ~18-20 fps (variable), we request 30 to allow smooth motion
r.impact: Higher fps = smoother motion but higher bitrate needed. Lower fps = choppier but less bandwidth.
r.alternatives: 15 fps (half, saves bandwidth), 8 fps (minimum for acceptable motion)

g.description: GOP (Group of Pictures) size in frames
g.critical: This determines MediaMTX segment size! GOP must be ≤ segment duration.
g.why_15: Creates 2 GOPs per 1-second MediaMTX segment, allows MediaMTX to cut segments cleanly
g.must_match_keyint_min: Set g = keyint_min for fixed GOP (no variable GOP)

keyint_min.description: Minimum interval between keyframes (I-frames) in frames
keyint_min.when_equal_to_g: Forces fixed GOP - keyframe every exactly 15 frames, no exceptions
keyint_min.why_critical: Variable GOP breaks LL-HLS segment alignment, causes buffering/stalls

b:v.alternatives: 400k (low quality, very compressed), 1200k (higher quality), 2000k+ (near-lossless but high bandwidth)

maxrate.when_equal_to_bv: Creates CBR (Constant Bitrate) - bitrate stays near 800k always
maxrate.why_cbr: Predictable bandwidth, prevents buffering, better for live streaming
maxrate.vbr_alternative: Set maxrate higher (e.g., 1200k) for VBR - variable bitrate, better quality on complex scenes

bufsize.impact: Larger buffer = smoother bitrate control but more delay. Smaller = faster response but more bitrate spikes.

scenecut.default: 40 - encoder inserts keyframe on scene changes (cuts, fades)
scenecut.why_0: Prevents unexpected keyframes that break fixed GOP, critical for LL-HLS segment alignment

min-keyint.description: Minimum keyframe interval (redundant with keyint_min but explicit)
min-keyint.why_set: Double ensures fixed GOP, some FFmpeg versions prioritize x264-params

open_gop.closed_gop: Each GOP is self-contained, can be decoded independently
open_gop.open_gop: GOP can reference frames from previous GOP, better compression but breaks seeking/segment boundaries
open_gop.why_0: Required for clean HLS segments - each segment must be independently decodable

force_key_frames.description: Expression to force keyframes at specific times

breakdown.expr: Signals this is an expression (not a simple interval)

force_key_frames.redundant_but_safe: With g=15 and scenecut=0, this should be automatic, but explicit forcing guarantees timing

vf.why_set_anyway: Explicit scaling ensures consistent output even if camera resolution changes

enabled.why_false: Audio adds ~64-128kbps + encoding overhead + potential sync issues. Disabled for minimum latency testing.
enabled.to_enable: Set to true and configure codec/bitrate/rate/channels below

codec.aac: Standard for web, good quality, universal support
codec.alternatives: mp3 (older, wider compatibility), opus (better quality, newer browsers)

rate.description: Audio sample rate in Hz (only used if enabled=true)

channels.2: Stereo - if camera has stereo mic

rtsp_input.description: FFmpeg parameters for reading camera's RTSP stream (Camera → FFmpeg)

rtsp_transport.description: Transport protocol for receiving from camera
rtsp_transport.udp: Lower latency, allows some packet loss, preferred for local network
rtsp_transport.tcp: Higher latency but guaranteed delivery, use if UDP has corruption (RTP: bad cseq errors)
rtsp_transport.note: This is INPUT transport (Camera→FFmpeg), separate from ll_hls.publisher.rtsp_transport (FFmpeg→MediaMTX)

timeout.why_5s: Prevents FFmpeg from hanging indefinitely if camera stops responding
timeout.too_high: Takes longer to detect dead cameras

analyzeduration.description: Maximum time to analyze stream before starting playback (microseconds = 1 second)
analyzeduration.tradeoff: Lower = faster start but may misdetect codecs. Higher = slower start but more reliable detection.
analyzeduration.why_1s: Balance between startup speed and codec detection accuracy

probesize.description: Amount of data to read for stream analysis (bytes = 1 MB)
probesize.tradeoff: Smaller = faster start but incomplete info. Larger = slower but complete stream info.
probesize.why_1mb: Sufficient for RTSP streams without excessive startup delay

use_wallclock_as_timestamps.description: Use system clock time instead of stream timestamps
use_wallclock_as_timestamps.why_1: Some cameras have unreliable/broken timestamps. System clock is more consistent for live streaming.
use_wallclock_as_timestamps.downside: Ignores actual frame timing from camera, assumes constant framerate

fflags.nobuffer: Minimizes input buffering for lower latency
fflags.alternative_genpts: +genpts to generate PTS (presentation timestamps) if stream lacks them - used on some Reolink cameras with UDP

rtsp_output.description: FFmpeg parameters for app-generated HLS (NOT USED when stream_type=LL_HLS)
rtsp_output.when_used: Only if you change stream_type from LL_HLS to HLS - then app generates HLS directly instead of using MediaMTX
rtsp_output.purpose: Fallback/comparison config to test this camera with regular HLS pipeline
rtsp_output.note: These settings are ignored for LL_HLS streams - see ll_hls section instead

map.0:a?: First audio stream if present (not in current config)

rtsp_output.various_encoding_params: Similar to ll_hls.video but different values - see those descriptions

f.HLS: HTTP Live Streaming - creates .m3u8 playlist + .ts segments

hls_time.why_0.5: Shorter segments = lower latency, but more overhead

hls_list_size.description: Number of segments to keep in playlist
hls_list_size.why_1: Minimal playlist for live streaming, removes old segments immediately

hls_flags.independent_segments: Each segment can be decoded independently
hls_flags.append_list: Updates playlist in-place instead of rewriting
hls_flags.split_by_time: Cut segments by time, not keyframes (may require transcoding)

hls_fmp4_init_resend.why: Allows mid-stream joining without waiting for start

hls_segment_type.mpegts: MPEG-TS - older, more compatible, lower overhead

hls_fmp4_init_filename.purpose: Contains codec info, required before playing media segments

movflags.frag_keyframe: Start new fragment at each keyframe

hls_delete_threshold.description: Delete segments when count exceeds list_size by this amount

hidden.description: Whether to hide camera from main UI grid
hidden.true: Hidden cameras still accessible but not displayed by default

ui_health_monitor.description: Enable frame-based health monitoring for this camera in browser
ui_health_monitor.true: UI checks for blank/frozen frames and attempts restart
ui_health_monitor.false: Disable if health monitor causes false positives (some cameras had this issue)
ui_health_monitor.see_also: ui_health_global_settings in cameras.json for system-wide health monitor config

known_issues.udp_publisher_freezing: ll_hls.publisher.rtsp_transport=udp causes stream to freeze within minutes. MediaMTX returns 404. FFmpeg process may die silently. Root cause unknown. TCP is only stable option.
known_issues.latency_degradation: Latency starts at ~2s but increases to 4-5s over hours of continuous streaming. Possibly MediaMTX segment buffer accumulation or TCP buffering.
known_issues.tcp_latency_penalty: Using TCP for publisher adds 1-2s compared to UDP, but UDP is unstable so we accept the penalty.
known_issues.regular_hls_faster: Ironically, regular HLS (rtsp_output config) currently has LOWER latency than LL-HLS due to transcoding overhead + TCP buffering. This is backwards and needs optimization.
